# EKEEL — Empowering Knowledge Extraction to Empower Learners

**EKEEL** is a research project funded under **PRIN 2022 PNRR** (National Research Program), aimed at developing advanced technologies for **autonomous knowledge extraction from video lectures** and leveraging this knowledge for **reusable educational support services**. ([ekeel.dibris.unige.it](https://ekeel.dibris.unige.it/))

---

## Funding and Program

- **Funding Program:** PRIN 2022 PNRR – P20227PEPK  
- **Grant:** CUP B53D23026030001  
- **Project Period:** December 2023 – February 2026 
- **Funding Bodies:** Italian Ministry of University and Research (MUR) - NextGenerationUE

---

## Project Overview

The project focuses on:

- Using **Artificial Intelligence (AI)** and **Natural Language Processing (NLP)** techniques to automatically understand the content of video lectures.  
- Extracting key concepts and **prerequisite relationships** between them to build an **educational Knowledge Graph**.  
- Providing structured information that can be reused in educational services and learning tools.

The approach integrates **deep learning models, text, image, and video analysis** to represent the dynamics of concepts presented in lectures.

---

## Main Objectives

- **Extract meaningful knowledge from video-based educational content.**  
- **Build representations of concepts and prerequisite relations** for personalized learning.  
- **Support high-performing digital education ecosystems**, in line with the Digital Education Action Plan 2021–27.

---


## Partners and Team

The project is coordinated by the **University of Genoa – DIBRIS**, with the contribution of researchers and project staff.

Key personnel include:

- Project Coordinator: **Ilaria Torre** (University of Genoa)  
- Scientific Lead (CNR‑ILC): **Felice Dell’Orletta**
